meta.structured_descriptor:
KEEP:
- Imiliya
- Kapilvastu (use canonical "Kapilvastu District")
- Lumbini

RESTORE:
- explicit type/answer clue: "pilgrimage site" / "temple"
- site-specific title/alias: "Maya Devi Temple" (title-level clue)
- discriminating descriptors: "sacred garden", "monastic zone", "UNESCO"
- canonical place relation: Imiliya → (in) Kapilvastu District → (near) Lumbini
- alternate locality spellings/names to recover nearby candidates

RETAIN:
- ambiguity among local settlements near Lumbini/Kapilvastu (candidate set: Jayanagar, Vidhyanagar, Taulihawa, Hariharpur, Motipur, etc.)
- boundary ambiguity between "Lumbini" as the site vs. Kapilvastu district/local villages
- multiple plausible answer types (pilgrimage site vs. village/settlement vs. temple)

DROP:
- broad country-level token "Nepal" (too general for hop2)
- noisy proximity/coordinate heuristics ("coordinates proximity") if they swamp title signals
- peripheral small-settlement noise when it distracts from site/temple signals (e.g., transient hits on Jayanagar/Vidhyanagar)

STYLE:
- compact BM25 style: 3–6 high-value tokens, bag-of-words, no stopwords
- prioritize canonical place names + answer-type + 1–2 discriminators; allow OR for locality candidates
- example pattern form (not a final query): [Imiliya] [Kapilvastu District] [Lumbini] [pilgrimage site|temple] [Maya Devi|sacred garden|UNESCO]

meta.structured_descriptor:
KEEP:
"Henry Green", "Henry Vincent Yorke", novelist, biography, occupations, career roles

RESTORE:
pen-name / birth-name relation (aka / born as), disambiguator "(novelist)" or title-type clue, explicit relation tokens linking biography -> occupations -> career roles, example novel-title cue (e.g., known work of the author) as a recovery anchor

RETAIN:
ambiguity between name variants and other "Henry" entities (Henry Green vs Henry Yorke vs other Henrys), candidate set of literary-person entities and their works (author pages, book titles), avoid prematurely collapsing to publisher/place/organization senses

DROP:
G. K. Chesterton and his occupations, Henry Holt and Company, Henry T. Botsford House, Bill Henry, Henry Nehemiah Nickerson, Sarah Dorsey, publishers/places/engineer/baseball-player side entities and other non-author Henrys

STYLE:
Compact BM25-friendly form using quoted name variants plus a small set of role/type tokens. Example pattern: ("Henry Green" OR "Henry Vincent Yorke") "biography" occupations career roles novelist — minimal punctuation, keep phrases quoted, OR only for name variants, other terms as bag-of-words.
